mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when UNRATE rises from previous month over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of cases, or average return is not significantly negative (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in both the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over two consecutive months indicate stagflation, a combination of rising inflation and economic weakness that severely depresses corporate margins and equity prices. This interaction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 191.3, UNRATE at 5.4% in recent months
failure_condition: During periods of simultaneous CPIAUCSL and UNRATE increases, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive in over 40% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation environment where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3%, an increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals an overheating economy that forces the Fed to tighten further, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL YoY growth estimated from levels
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 3%, PAYEMS increases are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of instances over a walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over the past three months indicates expanding manufacturing activity and rising corporate profits, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises over a 3-month period, 60-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or average return is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1800 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment, which is a leading indicator of economic recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST drops below 1800, subsequent 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in both retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) over the same month signals robust consumer demand and improving labor market, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 352138, PAYEMS at 132843 in recent months
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS increase month-over-month, 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of cases, or average return is not significantly positive (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low unemployment regime where UNRATE is below 5%, a further decline in UNRATE over the month signals extreme labor market tightness that leads to wage inflation pressures, prompting the Fed to tighten, eventually resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.4% as of 2005-02, but regime below 5% is rare at this point
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5% and declining, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative over a 3-year walk-forward window.
